edl.author: OPER
edl.screen_text: A SET OF WALLS CAVING INWARD
edl.sound: seething and simmering in a solitude of crowds and smiles

voiceover.text: It was the type of silent scream a trapped lover makes to escape: a pending marriage, a concussion, a set of walls caving inward.

audio.vo_mode: caption-only (poem audio lives in the master mp3)

edl.author: OPER
edl.screen_text: TRYING TO SEE IN THE DARK
edl.sound: a winless insomnia; the room's pulse in the ears

voiceover.text: An emergency call while tossing and turning — the silent call a man makes to the mirror after midnight, to digest a truth.

audio.vo_mode: caption-only (poem audio lives in the master mp3)

edl.author: OPER
edl.sound: the black opens; distant engine surfacing

voiceover.text: A winless insomnia. An epiphany of a road winding down. My eyes dilate — and we go through.

audio.vo_mode: caption-only (poem audio lives in the master mp3)

edl.author: OPER
edl.sound: snow ticking on glass; an engine slowing on the winding road

voiceover.text: I was six, I recall, when the ghost of a dead and love-less seven-year-old appeared, and shared that my parents' marriage wouldn't last. A warm, heavy breeze in winter, where a child is left home alone.

audio.vo_mode: caption-only (poem audio lives in the master mp3)

edl.author: OPER
edl.relation: open: the ghost and the argument
edl.screen_text: TWO SOULS ON A ROAD WINDING DOWN
edl.sound: a muffled argument through winter glass; a whisper close to the ear

voiceover.text: Curtains pulled, and blinds up as far and as wise as possible — to see flashing lights, and the whispering screams of two souls on a road winding down.

audio.vo_mode: caption-only (poem audio lives in the master mp3)

edl.author: OPER
edl.screen_text: NO TEARS - NO LOVE TO GIVE
edl.sound: one jangle in a quiet room; then glass, everywhere

voiceover.text: And no tears, and no fucks, and no love to give. The ghost hands me a tambourine — and I throw it through the window.

audio.vo_mode: caption-only (poem audio lives in the master mp3)

motion_cut.title: FLASHING LIGHTS
motion_cut.principle: Film 02 of the WYGWYL suite, in the inverted register: the whole film sits on CLR 7 and everything visible is carved light (0-2 beams/ghost/glass, 3 night sky, 4-5 dim matter, 6-7 silhouette). The child is only a cutout against the window's grey; the ghost is made of glow and never quite touches the floor; the parents are two 2px heads behind a lit rear window; the whole memory is entered through a dilating pupil that swallows the frame. The tambourine exits frame toward the moon — the handoff object to Film 03.

audio_lock.structure: title card = drone header; motion beats = poem body; end card = drone tail

motifs.the-sweep: light crossing a room with nobody to catch it
motifs.the-pupil: a heartbeat slowing as it widens
motifs.the-whisper: consonants only, close to the ear

sound_map.recurring: headlights sweeping wallpaper at argument angles
sound_map.silence: the room after the ghost dissolves
sound_map.final: the last jingle decaying over ODE TO TEACH.

cultural_system.worldview_description: A world saturated with sensory stimuli that often conceal or amplify underlying emotional distress and past trauma. The world feels chaotic, overwhelming, and laden with the echoes of past hurts, leading to emotional detachment. The general order of existence is one of inevitable loss and a constant struggle against internal and external pressures, where happiness is a transient illusion. The raw, unfiltered sensory and emotional experiences of the narrator are deeply authentic and reflective of a harsh reality. Reality is a series of intense, often painful moments that leave lasting scars, making emotional distance a practical survival strategy.
cultural_system.ethos_prescription: Be alert to the hidden pain behind superficial appearances; recognize the impact of past events. You should brace for impact, understand the inevitability of pain, and seek to minimize emotional vulnerability. Accept loss as an intrinsic part of life; build resilience against emotional devastation. The experience of trauma and subsequent desensitization is a legitimate and often necessary response to life's harshness. There is no escape from life's inherent pain; the most realistic approach is to endure and become numb to its impact.
cultural_system.sacred_symbols: Headlights; Sirens; Flashing lights; Walls caving; Exploding stars (concussion); Broken rings/chains; Crumbled paper hearts; VHS static; Ghost children; Ticking clocks; Empty roads.
cultural_system.moods: Anxiety; Helplessness; Despair; Desensitization; Child-like fear; Desire to escape; Self-preservation; Emotional numbness.
cultural_system.worldview_propositions: Life as a relentless journey; Innocence lost; Relationships as fragile/doomed; Time as a relentless progression toward decay; Solitude as a consequence.
cultural_system.mood_stabilizers: Use of direct sensory ekphrasis ("Stars explode behind eyelids", "Sound waves flatten to a line"); Repetitive imagery (walls caving, roads winding); Narrator's personal reflection ("I was six—").
cultural_system.totalizing_framework: Totalizing framework of a world where emotional and structural integrity are constantly undermined by life's "flashing lights" |.

poem_film.title: FLASHING LIGHTS
poem_film.series: WHERE YOU GO WHEN YOU LEAVE - PART 2 OF 3: FUTURE
poem_film.logline: Floating in black between caving walls, a poet's eyes dilate until the pupil swallows the frame — and inside is age six: a window, a winding road, arguing headlights, a ghost with a tambourine, and the first throw.
poem_film.thesis: The silent scream and the silent call are the same instrument; the tambourine is the first time the poet answers noise with noise.
poem_film.structure: 8 beats: field title / floating in black / eyes dilating / through the eyes / I was six / the ghost and the argument / the tambourine / ODE TO TEACH.

characters.POET: adrift in thin light; then a six-year-old drawn only as a black cutout against the window
characters.EX: curled in distress, faceless, drifting up and away — unreachable
characters.GHOST CHILD: shade-2 glow, feet dissolving above the floor; flickers in, whispers, dissolves, returns, hugs
characters.PARENTS: two 2px heads behind a lit rear window; their screams drawn as zigzags of light

motifs.light: the only ink: sweeps, cones, glow, cracks — the dark is the paper
motifs.the-pupil: dilation as door; the film passes through an eye to reach the past
motifs.the-road: winds down inside the window and inside the phrase
motifs.the-tambourine: arrives as a ring of light; exits toward the moon, bound for the chapel of Film 03

poem_film.source: WYGWYL assembly treatment R6-4-25, 'Flashing Lights' poem field
poem_film.final_image: ODE TO TEACH. under a dim tambourine, all carved from black
poem_film.handoff: the tambourine leaves frame upper-right at speed — Film 03 opens on it tumbling
